title: 10 medicinal constituents of Taraxacum × mesohalobium (dune daisy)

constituents_list.constituent_desc: Taraxacum × mesohalobium inulin is a type of fructan, a soluble dietary fiber extracted from the roots and leaves of this plant species. Inulin has been studied for its potential health benefits, including prebiotic effects, antioxidant properties, and ability to lower cholesterol levels. It is often used as a natural sweetener and thickening agent in food products due to its unique properties and versatility.

Taraxacum × mesohalobium luteolin is a hybrid plant species that belongs to the Asteraceae family. It is a rare and endangered species found in coastal sand dunes of Japan and Korea. The plant has yellow flowers with black centers and is adapted to survive in salty environments. Luteolin, a flavonoid compound present in the plant, exhibits antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, making it valuable for potential medicinal applications.

Taraxacum × mesohalobium taraxasterol is a rare and unique compound found in this plant species. Taraxasterol is a type of triterpenoid that has been isolated from the leaves and stems of Taraxacum × mesohalobium. It is believed to have antioxidant properties and has shown potential in the development of new medicines.

Taraxacum × mesohalobium kaempferol is a unique compound found in this plant species. Kaempferol is a flavonoid, a type of plant pigment responsible for the yellow and orange colors often seen in flowers. This compound has been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, making it of interest in potential applications in medicine and food technology.

Taraxacum × mesohalobium quercetin is a natural flavonoid compound extracted from its flowers. It has been found to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, making it a potential therapeutic agent for various diseases. Quercetin has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, reduce oxidative stress, and exhibit antimicrobial activity against certain bacteria and fungi. Its bioactive compounds have also been explored as natural additives in food and cosmetic products.

Taraxacum × mesohalobium chlorogenic acid is a type of bioactive compound found in its leaves and flowers. It has been shown to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may contribute to the plant's traditional use in herbal medicine for treating various ailments. Additionally, chlorogenic acid has been found to have antimicrobial effects, making it a potential natural preservative.

Taraxacum × mesohalobium cynarine is a rare and unique species of flowering plant. It is a hybrid of Taraxacum officinale and Cymareta scabrella, resulting in a peculiar appearance with bright yellow flowers and frilled petals. This halophilic plant thrives in salt-tolerant environments, often found in coastal dunes, estuaries, and salt marshes. Its peculiarities make it an interesting subject for botanists and enthusiasts alike.

Taraxacum × mesohalobium galactomannans is a unique polysaccharide compound found in the cell walls of this halophilic plant. It is characterized by its ability to retain water and provide structural support despite exposure to high salt concentrations. These galactomannans play a crucial role in the survival of T. × mesohalobium, enabling it to thrive in environments with high salinity.

Taraxacum × mesohalobium polyacetylenes is a unique class of compounds found in this species. These polyacetylenes have been shown to possess significant bioactive properties, including antimicrobial, antioxidant, and anti-inflammatory activities. Research has demonstrated that these compounds exhibit potent cytotoxic effects against various cancer cell lines, making them potential leads for the development of novel anticancer drugs.

Taraxacum × mesohalobium fatty acids is a unique combination of lipids found in the seeds and leaves of this coastal plant. These fatty acids have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them potential candidates for treating various diseases. The specific composition of Taraxacum × mesohalobium fatty acids is characterized by high levels of oleic acid, linoleic acid, and palmitic acid, which contribute to their therapeutic effects.

intro: Taraxacum × mesohalobium has active constituents such as sesquiterpenoids, flavonoids, and alkaloids. These compounds exhibit medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The constituents can improve people's lives by providing relief from respiratory issues, wound healing, and even cancer treatment. For instance, the sesquiterpenoids have been shown to alleviate symptoms of bronchial asthma, while the flavonoids may help combat against cardiovascular diseases and certain types of cancer.
intro_desc: Taraxacum × mesohalobium has active constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids. These compounds are responsible for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The flavonoids in dune daisy can improve cardiovascular health by reducing inflammation and improving blood vessel function. Additionally, its terpenoids have been shown to have antimicrobial properties, which can help protect against infections. These medicinal properties can improve people's lives by reducing the risk of chronic diseases.
